sections.paragraph: m. Gen 4:21 Job 21:12 30:31 , and עֻגָב Psa 150:4 (where many MSS. and printed editions have עֻגָּב), tibia, fistula, syrinx, pipe, reed, as rightly given by the Hebrew interpreters. Targ. אַבּוּבָא a pipe (Syr. ܐܢܒܘܒܐ, Zab. ܐܡܒܘܒܐ; whence ambubaja, i.e. tibicina, Hor.); Jerome, organon, i.e. a double or manifold pipe, an instrument composed of many pipes. In Dan 3:5, 10 , 15 , the Hebrew translator uses it for the Chald. סוּמְפֹּנְיָה, which see. The root is עָגַב, probably with the primary signification of breathing, blowing.
